message:
fix(vfs): cross-platform libc type compatibility and io_uring panic resilience

unix.rs:
- Remove redundant `.into()` calls on libc::F_UNLCK, libc::F_RDLCK, and
  libc::F_WRLCK constants passed to posix_lock(). On macOS (aarch64-apple-darwin),
  these constants are i32 while the function expects i32, so the .into() calls
  compiled on Linux but failed on macOS with "type annotations needed" because
  the From impl is ambiguous when source and target types match. Removing .into()
  lets the compiler resolve the types directly on both platforms.
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] to PENDING_BYTE, RESERVED_BYTE, SHARED_FIRST, and
  SHARED_SIZE constants that are defined for documentation/spec parity with
  SQLite's os_unix.c but not yet referenced in the advisory-lock-based
  implementation.

uring.rs:
- Wrap io_uring read and write operations in std::panic::catch_unwind() to
  prevent panics inside the uring-fs runtime (e.g. from poisoned mutex or
  pollster re-entrancy) from unwinding through the VFS layer. On panic, the
  error is converted to FrankenError::Io with a descriptive message.
- Change VfsFile::read() and VfsFile::write() from if/else (uring vs fallback)
  to try-uring-then-fallback: if io_uring succeeds, return immediately;
  otherwise fall through to the standard Unix I/O path. This makes the uring
  backend a transparent accelerator rather than a hard requirement, improving
  robustness on kernels where io_uring is restricted (seccomp, older kernels).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
